name: Anjali Hasta
description: Hold Pataka in both the hands & join the palms to show Anjali Hasta.
viniyoga_translation: To salute God, Teacher & the Learned hold the Anjali Hasta above the head, in front of the face & in front of chest respectively.

detection_rules.connection: Palms pressed flat against each other.

name: Kapota Hasta
description: In Anjali Hasta, only the borders of the hands are joined(Palm should not touch one another) to show Kapota Hasta.
viniyoga_translation: To make promise, Speak to the teacher, To be Polite, To Agree this hasta is used.

detection_rules.connection: Base, sides, and fingertips touching, palms slightly separated creating a hollow cavity.

name: Karkata Hasta
description: Bring the fingers of both the hands between one another to show Karkata Hasta.
viniyoga_translation: To show Crowd, Thick articles, Blowing of Shankha, Stretching Limbs, Bending the branches of a tree this hasta is used.

detection_rules.connection: Fingers of both hands interlocked natively inward towards the back of the hands.

name: Swastika Hasta
description: Hold Pataka Hands & cross the hands at the wrist so that the hands are opposite to each other to shoe Swastika Hasta.
viniyoga_translation: This hasta is used to show Aligater, To Talk in fear, to Argument and to praise this hasta is used.

detection_rules.connection: Hands crossed symmetrically at the wrists form an 'X' shape.

name: Dola Hasta
description: Hold Pataka Hasta, stretch the arms & keep the hasta upside down along the hip line to show Dola Hasta.
viniyoga_translation: This hasta is used in the beginning of a dance.

detection_rules.connection: No physical connection. Arms fully stretched out beside the hips, palms facing downwards.

name: Pushpaputa Hasta
description: Hold Sarpasheersha in both the hands & join them at the wrist to show Pushpaputa Hasta.
viniyoga_translation: To show Lamp Offering, Children, Accept Fruits, Offering to the Sun in the evenings, Chant Holy prayers This hasta is used.

detection_rules.connection: Sides of the hands/wrists are pressed together to form a bowl (receiving gesture).

name: Utsanga Hasta
description: Hold Mrugasheersha Hasta in boh the hands, cross the hands, touch opposite shoulders to show Utsanga Hasta.
viniyoga_translation: To Embrace, To show shyness, To show one's body, To deciplinne children Utsanga hasta is used.

detection_rules.connection: Arms crossed across the chest, right hand on left shoulder, left hand on right shoulder.

name: Shivalinga Hasta
description: Hold Ardhachandra Hasta in the left hand(palm up), keep shikhara Hasta in right hand & place it on the left hand to show Shivalinga Hasta.
viniyoga_translation: This Hasta is used to show Shivalinga (Lord Shiva).

detection_rules.connection: Right Shikhara is vertically resting firmly on top of the flat open left Ardhachandra.

name: Katakavardhana Hasta
description: Hold Katakamukha Hasta in both hands, cross the hands at the wrist to show Katakavardhana Hasta.
viniyoga_translation: To show Coronation, To worship and to show weddings this hasta is used.

detection_rules.connection: Hands crossed vertically at the wrists.

name: Kartareeswastika Hasta
description: Hold Kartareemukha Hasta in both the hands & cross the hands at the wrist to show Kartareeswastika Hasta.
viniyoga_translation: To show the branches of a tree, tip of Mountains, Trees This hasta is used.

detection_rules.connection: Hands crossed vertically at the wrists.

name: Shakata Hasta
description: Leave the thumb & the middle fingers in Bhramara Hasta. Hold like this in both the hands & cross at the wrist to show Shakata Hasta. Another way of showing Shakata Hasta is to cross the Arala Hastas at the wrist.
viniyoga_translation: This hasta is used to show Demons.

detection_rules.connection: Hands crossed at the wrist (showing bulging eyes/demon teeth).

name: Shankha Hasta
description: Hold the Left thumb with the last three fingers of the right hand, stretch the other fingers of the left hand, stretch & touch the thumb & index fingers of the right hand with the stretched fingers of the left hand to show Shankha Hasta.
viniyoga_translation: This hasta is used to show Conch.

detection_rules.left_hand: Four fingers straight, thumb extended outward.
detection_rules.right_hand: Grasping left thumb tightly with lower three fingers, thumb and index stretched upwards.
detection_rules.connection: Right hand wraps around left thumb, left fingers touch the extended right thumb, forming a conch shape.

name: Chakra Hasta
description: Hold Ardhachandra hasta in both the hands, place them one above the other like a Plus(+) mark to show Chakra Hasta.
viniyoga_translation: This Hasta is used to show Chakra, the weapon of Lord Vishnu.

detection_rules.connection: Palms placed one vertically above the other horizontally perpendicularly to form a Plus (+) or wheel shape.

name: Samputa Hasta
description: Hold Chakra Hasta, fold the thumb & little fingers in both the hands to show Samputa Hasta.
viniyoga_translation: To cover things and to show the sacred box in which the idols are placed this Samputa Hasta is used.

detection_rules.connection: Placed one above the other perpendicularly forming a closed box.

name: Paasha Hasta
description: Hold Suchi Hasta in both hands, bend the index finger a little & join these fingers like a chain to show Paasha Hasta.
viniyoga_translation: To show Playful Quarrel, Rope, Chains this hasta is used.

detection_rules.connection: The two bent index fingers interlock with each other creating a chain or hook.

name: Keelaka Hasta
description: Hold Mrugasheersha Hasta in both hands, bend the little finger a little & join these fingers like a chain to show Keelaka Hasta.
viniyoga_translation: To show friendly talk this hasta is used.

detection_rules.connection: The two extended pinky fingers interlock with each other creating a chain.

name: Matsya Hasta
description: Hold Pataka hasta in both the hands, place them one above the other, stretch the thumb a bit (like fins of fish) to show Matsya Hasta.
viniyoga_translation: This hasta is used to show Fish.

detection_rules.right_hand: Pataka Hasta (facing down resting on top of left)
detection_rules.connection: Right palm rests onto the back of the left hand, both thumbs extend outward to act as fins.

name: Koorma Hasta
description: Opposite of Chakra Hasta is Koorma Hasta i.e Stretch the thumb & little fingers & fold the other fingers in Chakra Hasta to show Koorma Hasta.
viniyoga_translation: This Hasta is used to show Turtle, Tortoise

detection_rules.left_hand: Index, middle, ring fingers folded in; thumb and pinky stretched out.
detection_rules.right_hand: Index, middle, ring fingers folded in; thumb and pinky stretched out.
detection_rules.connection: Palms stacked vertically over each other, the 4 extended thumbs/pinkies act as the tortoise legs.

name: Varaha Hasta
description: Hold Mrugasheersha hasta in both hands, place them one above the other to show Varaha Hasta.
viniyoga_translation: This Hasta is used to Show Boar (Wild Pig)

detection_rules.connection: Right palm rests on back of the left hand as they point identically.

name: Garuda Hasta
description: Hold Ardhachandra in both the hands, turn them & hold them with the thumbs to show Garuda Hasta.
viniyoga_translation: This Hasta is used to show a bird called Garuda.

detection_rules.connection: Palms turned toward body, thumbs firmly interlocked, acting as wings spanning outward.

name: Nagabandha Hasta
description: Hold Sarpasheersha in both the hands & cross them at the wrist to show Nagabandha Hasta.
viniyoga_translation: To show Snakes, Creeper Chamber, Atharvana Veda Shlokas this hasta is used.

detection_rules.connection: Crossed solidly at the wrists, pointing to snake hoods twisting together.

name: Khatva Hasta
description: Hold Chatura Hasta in both hands, place the hands one above the other, stretch the index fingers down to show Khatva Hasta.
viniyoga_translation: This Hasta is used to show Bed.

detection_rules.left_hand: Chatura Hasta (index finger extended straight downwards)
detection_rules.right_hand: Chatura Hasta (index finger extended straight downwards)
detection_rules.connection: Hands placed horizontally side by side over each other, the downward index fingers forming the legs of the cot/bed.

name: Bherundha Hasta
description: Hold Kapitha Hasta & cross them at the wrist to show Bherundha Hasta.
viniyoga_translation: To show a bird couple called Bherundha this hasta is used.

detection_rules.connection: Crossed closely at the wrists pointing upwards.

name: Avahittha Hasta
description: Hold Alapadma in both the hands, cross them at the wrist, place them near chest to show Avahitha Hasta.
viniyoga_translation: To show Love, Catching the ball, Breasts Avahittha Hasta is used.

detection_rules.connection: Crossed at the wrists slightly separated holding near the chest area pointing towards the shoulders.
